title: cerastium decalvans var. isophyllum
intro: Cerastium decalvans var. isophyllum, also known as a type of toadflax or sticky mouse-ear, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stop bleeding, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Cerastium decalvans var. isophyllum include its potential use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. It is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil to be used topically, and it can also be dried and made into capsules or tinctures for internal use.
